items.summary: Create a persistent floating music player component (frontend/src/components/FloatingMusicPlayer.tsx) fixed to the bottom-right corner of all pages. The player must: (1) embed and play YouTube tracks via the YouTube IFrame API in a hidden iframe; (2) include a pre-defined Sonic track rotation in order: Angel Island Zone Act 1, Angel Island Zone Act 2, Chemical Plant Zone, Green Hill Zone, Emerald Hill Zone, Ice Cap Zone, Flying Battery Zone, Hydrocity Zone — each mapped to a real YouTube video ID; (3) render left (⬅️) and right (➡️) arrow buttons to navigate to the previous/next track, wrapping around at the ends; (4) render an On/Off toggle button to enable or disable music playback; (5) render a volume slider from 0 to 100 with left/right nudge buttons (each nudge adjusts volume by 5 units); (6) auto-loop the current track when it ends; (7) persist current track index, volume level, and on/off state in sessionStorage so the player state survives navigation between pages within the session; (8) display the current track name above the controls.
Mount the FloatingMusicPlayer component in the root App layout (frontend/src/App.tsx or frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx) so it renders persistently on all pages regardless of route.

Create a new Settings page (frontend/src/pages/SettingsPage.tsx) accessible from the main navigation to all authenticated users. The page must include a 'Background Music' section that: (1) lists all tracks in the Sonic rotation (same 8 tracks as the floating player) with a 'Select' button per track to set it as the active track; (2) highlights the currently active track; (3) provides an 'Import YouTube URL' input field and an 'Add Track' button — when a valid YouTube URL is submitted, it is parsed for the video ID and appended to the user's track rotation for the session; (4) includes a volume slider (0–100) and on/off toggle mirrored from the floating player state (changes here update the floating player and vice versa via shared sessionStorage state); (5) displays an error if an invalid or empty YouTube URL is submitted.
Add a 'Settings' navigation link in the main app header/navbar (frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx) that routes to the new Settings page. This link must be visible to all authenticated users.
Register the Settings page route in the TanStack Router configuration (frontend/src/App.tsx) at a path such as '/settings', wrapped in an AuthGuard so only authenticated users can access it.
